Name: Ragnarök, the Doom of the Gods
Effect: Put this aside at the start of the game. The first time there are 8 or more deities or giants in play, Ragnarök happens at the end of that turn. Ragnarök: Banish all deities and giants.
Anecdote: 'Brothers will fight and kill each other. An axe-age, a sword-age, shields are riven; a wind-age, a wolf-age, before the world goes down.'

Name: Lif and Lifthrasir
Anecdote: 'Two of mankind hid in Hoddmimir's wood, and the morning dew was their food, and from them the world was peopled again.'

Name: Valkyries
Effect: On enter: Revive a being with a cost of [2] or less.
Anecdote: 'Over the field of the slain they ride, choosing who falls and who wakes at evening in the hall of the chosen.'

Name: Gjallarhorn
Effect: On enter: If there are 5 or more deities or giants in play, Ragnarök happens at the end of this turn.
Anecdote: 'Heimdall raises the horn and blows with all his might; its blast is heard in every world. The gods wake and meet in council.'

Name: Heimdall, the Watcher
Effect: On enter: Draw 'Gjallarhorn' from your deck.
Anecdote: 'He needs less sleep than a bird, sees a hundred leagues by night as by day, and hears the grass growing on the earth and the wool on the sheep.'

Name: Frigg, the Foreseeing
Effect: On enter: Look at the top 3 cards of your deck. Put them back in any order.
Anecdote: 'She knows all fates, though she speaks none of them. She wept, and did not say what she had known.'

Name: Einherjar, the Chosen Slain
Anecdote: 'Each day they arm and fight in the great courtyard and cut each other down; by evening they ride home whole and sit together to mead.'

Name: Loki, Breaker of Bonds
Effect: On enter: The next monster you play this turn costs [2] less.
Anecdote: 'Bound with the guts of his own son, venom dripping on his brow — when the bonds break, he comes steering the ship of the dead.'

Name: Vidar, the Silent
Anecdote: 'The silent god wears the thick shoe made of all the leather scraps of time; with it he stands on the wolf's lower jaw and tears him apart. And he lives on when the fire dies.'

Name: Baldr, the Shining
Effect: Protected from Ragnarök. On revive: Draw three cards.
Anecdote: 'All things wept for Baldr, save one. But after the fire and the flood he comes back from the world of the dead, and the fields grow unsown.'

Name: Thor, the Thunderer
Effect: Thor counts as two deities for defeating monsters.
Anecdote: 'He smashes the serpent's head, walks nine steps from it through the venom, and falls dead to the earth.'

Name: Odin, All-Father
Effect: On enter: Revive a deity with a cost of [3] or less.
Anecdote: 'The Allfather rides first in a helmet of gold to meet the wolf. He has known his fate since the seeress spoke, and rides anyway.'

Name: Fenrir, the Unbound
Effect: Cannot be played at a location where you have deities. Defeated when you have two or more deities here. Reward: Banish your strongest deity here, then draw three cards.
Anecdote: 'The fetter was smooth as silk, woven from the sound of a cat's footfall and the roots of a mountain. It held — until it did not. His jaws gape from earth to heaven.'

Name: Jörmungandr, the World-Serpent
Effect: Cannot be played at a location where you have deities. Defeated when you have two or more deities here. Reward: Banish your strongest deity here, then each opponent must banish two of their beings.
Anecdote: 'It lies in the sea around all lands and bites its own tail. When it lets go, the waves climb over the shore.'

Name: Surtr, the Fire-Giant
Effect: Cannot be played at a location where you have deities. Defeated when you have two or more deities here. Reward: Destroy all other beings at this location.
Anecdote: 'From the south he rides with his flaming sword, brighter than the sun; fire consumes the world-tree and the sea rises over the land.'
